articles.headline: TPG team, I Squared edge closer to Re Sustainability
articles.summary: I Squared Capital and a TPG-Canada Pension Plan consortium are final contenders to acquire a controlling stake in KKR-backed Re Sustainability Ltd. The deal is expected to value the Hyderabad-based waste management company at over $1.6 billion, with binding bids due in 10-15 days.

articles.headline: Haze over Tata Sons IPO as RBI defines ‘indirect receipts’
articles.summary: RBI's new definition of "indirect receipt of public funds" has clouded Tata Sons' mandatory listing fate. The definition was restored in an updated circular, crucial for Tata Sons as listed Tata companies own stakes, making it an indirect recipient of public funds.

articles.headline: Why hybrid vehicles are losing policy support in India
articles.summary: The Delhi government excluded hybrid vehicles from tax benefits, part of a national trend. States like Rajasthan, Chhattisgarh, and Uttar Pradesh also withdrew incentives, pushing for 100% electric vehicles. GST rates for large hybrids also increased, impacting sales.

articles.headline: Positive signals for Indian economy despite global disruptions
articles.summary: India's economic activity holds up well, with June's GST mop-up climbing 14% to ₹1.95 trillion, the fastest in over a year. Manufacturing PMI fell slightly to 54.2 but remains in expansion, reinforcing positive economic signals. Easing West Asian conflict also helps.

articles.headline: India’s passport mobility score ranks 66th globally
articles.summary: An Indian passport provides visa-free or visa-on-arrival entry to 72 destinations, ranking 66th globally. The external affairs ministry clarified it's a travel document, not conclusive citizenship proof. UAE, France, and Spain top the list.

articles.headline: Kotak Mahindra Bank faces challenges under new Chief
articles.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank MD and CEO Ashok Vaswani will not seek reappointment. His tenure faced regulatory setbacks, slowing profitability, high attrition, and muted shareholder returns. The bank's credit card base shrank after an RBI ban.

articles.headline: PMI indicates cooling prices, stalled growth in manufacturing
articles.summary: The June manufacturing PMI fell to a three-month low of 54.2, showing cooling inflationary pressures but slowing growth. Output and new orders had their weakest growth in four years. Input costs increased at the slowest pace since February.

articles.headline: Delhi HC: NSE is public authority under RTI Act
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court ruled that the National Stock Exchange (NSE) is a "public authority" under the Right to Information (RTI) Act, ending a 16-year legal battle. This allows greater transparency in its functioning and regulatory oversight.

articles.headline: Aurobindo Pharma’s investors await next growth phase
articles.summary: Aurobindo Pharma completed its acquisition of Lannett Company, Inc., USA, adding nearly $300 million to annual revenue. The deal, valued at $250 million, offers a turnaround opportunity by improving Lannett's underutilized manufacturing and product portfolio.

articles.headline: Indian equities underperform global bull run in H1 2026
articles.summary: Indian equities faced a turbulent first half of 2026, with the Nifty 50 declining 8.7%, its worst H1 since 2022. Geopolitical tensions, foreign selling, earnings downgrades, and AI concerns weighed on sentiment, contrasting with strong gains in other Asian markets.

articles.headline: AI, nuclear investments hide India’s capex slump
articles.summary: India Inc's new project announcements rose 30% to ₹14.5 trillion in April-June, but nuclear power and data centers account for three-fourths of this. Traditional sectors like manufacturing, mining, and transport services saw significant declines in investments.

articles.headline: Monsoon waiting: dry soil, empty wells, and a Plan C
articles.summary: Monsoon rains are delayed and scanty in Madhya Pradesh, impacting Kharif sowing. A 40% rainfall deficit in June nationwide has led to a 23% lower planting area year-on-year for crops like oilseeds and pulses. Farmers face potential yield losses and economic hardship.

articles.headline: MFs wary of Sebi’s proposal to allow celeb ads
articles.summary: Sebi's proposal to allow celebrity endorsements for mutual fund houses faces industry resistance, with executives warning it could encourage mis-selling and unfairly benefit larger asset managers. The move could also significantly increase advertising costs for all MFs.

articles.headline: OMCs under pressure to cut fuel prices as oil cools
articles.summary: Pressure is mounting on state-run oil marketing companies (OMCs) to cut petrol and diesel prices after private refiner Nayara Energy reduced its retail fuel prices. Global crude oil prices have fallen to pre-war levels, but OMCs' auto fuel prices remain high.

articles.headline: Government plans framework to regulate solar waste recycling
articles.summary: India is developing a national policy for solar module recycling to minimize environmental impact and recover materials. With solar projects ending by 2030, the government plans to mandate module recycling by 2029-30, engaging industry stakeholders.

articles.headline: Government to assess economic output of cities first time
articles.summary: The housing ministry has formed an inter-ministerial committee to develop a standardized methodology for estimating the City Economic Product (CEP) of Indian cities. This first-of-its-kind initiative aims to quantify urban economic contributions for evidence-based planning.

articles.headline: India, Japan to deepen ties on AI, chips during PM Takaichi’s visit
articles.summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's visit aims to deepen cooperation with India in AI, semiconductors, defence, and strategic ties. Discussions will focus on energy resilience, critical minerals, and digital collaboration amidst global geopolitical shifts.

articles.headline: AI included in top deck performance appraisals
articles.summary: Employers are integrating AI adoption as a parameter in performance appraisals for senior leadership, especially in banking and consulting. Business houses are rating 5-15% of senior leaders based on beneficial AI use, linking it to productivity gains and efficiency.

articles.headline: FSSAI pulls up beverage brands over ‘energy drink’ labels
articles.summary: FSSAI issued notices to several beverage manufacturers for misbranding and misleading claims using "energy drink" labels without official standards. It also objected to unpermitted functional/therapeutic claims like "stimulates mind" on these products.

articles.headline: Small LPG cylinders stare at a big problem
articles.summary: Demand for portable 5kg LPG cylinders, mainly used by migrant workers, has plunged by 80-90% after prices doubled following the West Asia war. The price hike has made them more expensive per kg than larger commercial cylinders.

articles.headline: State land authorities lose bankruptcy leverage
articles.summary: A change in law clarifies that land development authorities do not automatically get secured creditor status in insolvency processes, providing relief to banks and home buyers. This speeds up rescue of bankrupt real estate projects by reducing litigation.

articles.headline: Hyderabad airport plans new arrival fees
articles.summary: GMR-run Hyderabad airport proposed introducing an arrival fee for passengers for the first time while cutting departure fees by 23%. This tariff restructuring aims to fund its ₹13,975-crore expansion plan without changing overall passenger revenue significantly.

articles.headline: Young travellers, easier access fuel cruise demand
articles.summary: Cruise vacations are gaining popularity in India, shifting from niche luxury to mainstream, driven by young travellers and easier access. Operators like Cordelia Cruises are expanding capacity, and travel companies are deepening offerings to meet growing demand.

articles.headline: Digital, AI standards key to global trade: ICC’s Singhania
articles.summary: Harsh Pati Singhania, newly elected ICC chairman, states that digital standards and responsible AI rules are crucial for global trade amidst geopolitical shifts. He prioritizes digitizing trade documentation to simplify cross-border commerce for SMEs.

articles.headline: Zee promoter family to infuse ₹3,143 cr
articles.summary: The Goenka family, promoters of Zee Entertainment, proposed to invest up to ₹3,143.51 crore via fully convertible warrants, potentially raising their stake to nearly 25%. This move comes after a subdued FY26 for the company.

articles.headline: CtrlS faces CPP stake hike if targets slip
articles.summary: CtrlS Datacenters Pvt. Ltd faces a potential increase in Canada Pension Plan Investment Board's (CPP Investments) stake from 8.2% to 10.3% if execution milestones for its Hyderabad and Mumbai data center projects are missed.

articles.headline: Govt directs Meta to halt WhatsApp usernames rollout
articles.summary: MeitY directed Meta to halt the rollout of WhatsApp's username feature due to concerns over potential impersonation and cyberfraud. The government sought an explanation and warned of regulatory action under IT Act and Rules.

articles.headline: Accenture expands Pune workplace strategy
articles.summary: Accenture Solutions Pvt. Ltd leased 345,872 sq. ft. in Pune, part of its expansion strategy across Indian tech hubs. This follows significant leases in Hyderabad, Noida, and Kolkata, consolidating offices and bringing employees under one roof.

articles.headline: Regional studios diversify into multilingual content
articles.summary: Regional content studios, traditionally working in niche languages, are diversifying into Hindi and other non-Hindi languages. This strategy helps diversify risk and expand portfolios, leveraging OTT platforms and growing demand for diverse stories.

articles.headline: Alphabet’s $2 trillion gain turns ‘rock star’ into question mark
articles.summary: Alphabet's stock, despite doubling in 12 months, struggled recently, losing 6% in June. Investors are wary of high AI spending, potential equity dilution, and intense competition in the AI sector, causing its valuation to be questioned.

articles.headline: Xi hails party as force for global prosperity
articles.summary: Chinese leader Xi Jinping asserted the ruling party's role as a force for global progress and prosperity, backed by a strong military. He outlined ambitions for China to lead global affairs and achieve "national rejuvenation," including resolving the "Taiwan problem."

articles.headline: Ukraine’s missiles now reach almost half of Russia
articles.summary: Ukraine has expanded the range and intensity of its missile strikes deep inside Russia, triggering alerts across nearly half the country's regions this year. This highlights evolving tactics and long-range strike capabilities, bringing the war closer to home for Russians.

articles.headline: US lifts export curbs on Anthropic’s Fable 5 AI model
articles.summary: The US government removed foreign access restrictions on Anthropic PBC’s Fable 5 AI model after the startup resolved safety concerns. Anthropic deployed new classifiers to block cybersecurity tasks and agreed to work with the government on AI safety standards.

articles.headline: Trump made over $1 bn on crypto deals
articles.summary: Former President Trump reported a windfall of over $1 billion last year from crypto businesses, including royalties from his memecoin and proceeds from World Liberty Financial token sales. He also earned from real estate, licensing, and legal settlements.

articles.headline: Meta building cloud business to sell AI compute
articles.summary: Meta Platforms Inc. plans to develop a cloud infrastructure business to sell access to AI computing power and models. This new venture sets up competition with industry leaders like AWS and Azure, leveraging Meta's extensive AI infrastructure investments.

articles.headline: US, Iran hold technical talks on Venezuela peace deal
articles.summary: The US and Iran held indirect technical talks in Doha to agree on shipping flow through the Strait of Hormuz and secure a lasting ceasefire, based on a 14-point interim accord.

articles.headline: Venezuela earthquake death toll nears 2,000
articles.summary: The death toll from twin earthquakes in Venezuela last week rose to nearly 2,000, with almost 20,000 survivors in La Guaira. Over 10,000 people were injured, and 15,000 displaced.

articles.headline: Prudential-HCL wins Irdai approval for health insurance
articles.summary: India’s insurance regulator Irdai granted a certificate of registration to Prudential HCL Health Insurance Ltd, allowing it to begin health insurance operations in India as the country’s eighth standalone health insurer.

articles.headline: India aims to recycle 16K ships in 10 years
articles.summary: India plans to recycle nearly 16,000 ships over the next decade with an $8 billion financial commitment to shipbuilding and recycling. India and the EU reaffirmed cooperation in sustainable ship recycling.

articles.headline: Magnet Hospitals debuts with 8 hospitals
articles.summary: Dinesh Madhavan launched Magnet Hospitals, a new healthcare network with an initial portfolio of eight city hospitals and ₹224 crore investment. It aims to disrupt the corporate hospital model by offering equity stakes to doctors and nurses.

articles.headline: Cabinet clears UP highway, Delhi tunnel projects
articles.summary: CCEA approved two major road infrastructure projects worth over ₹14,000 crore: a ₹6,970-crore 8km, six-lane tunnel in Delhi and a ₹7,145-crore 242km access-controlled highway in Uttar Pradesh.

articles.headline: Foreign secretary Misri gets one-year extension
articles.summary: Foreign secretary Vikram Misri was granted a one-year extension until 14 July 2027. Misri, known for his expertise on China, was appointed to the post in June 2024.

articles.headline: IndiGo launches cabin-only fares
articles.summary: IndiGo introduced entry-level "lite fare" category for passengers travelling with only cabin baggage on direct domestic and international flights, allowing them to pay only for needed services.

articles.headline: Passenger vehicle sales up 25% in June
articles.summary: Passenger vehicle sales in India are estimated to have grown nearly 25% in June to about 400,000 units, led by Maruti Suzuki, Tata Motors, and Mahindra & Mahindra, despite concerns over the West Asia war and deficient monsoon.

articles.headline: TVS’s Norton bet enters test phase
articles.summary: TVS Motor Co. Ltd has booked nearly ₹2,000 crore in losses at its British premium motorcycle brand, The Norton Motorcycle Co., since acquiring it in 2020. These losses reflect TVS's investments in reviving the brand for a global relaunch.

articles.headline: AI skill can be your next multibagger investment
articles.summary: AI credentials are now essential in the job market, with courses ranging from ₹500 to ₹10 lakh. Industry leaders emphasize practical projects and workplace application over mere certifications, suggesting a "learning budget" and cautious investment.

articles.headline: The voice of the Minions finally understands them
articles.summary: Filmmaker Pierre Coffin, creator and chief practitioner of Minionese, discusses the evolution and challenges of the Minions franchise. He admits initial doubts about sequels but expresses enthusiasm for the latest film, 'Minions & Monsters,' where the characters become filmmakers in 1920s Hollywood.

articles.headline: Govt asks Meta to pause WhatsApp user names rollout over fraud risks
articles.summary: The IT Ministry issued a notice to Meta seeking a pause on WhatsApp’s 'user names' feature. The government is concerned that allowing users to hide phone numbers could facilitate impersonation, financial fraud, and the spread of misinformation. Meta must now seek regulatory approval and detail safeguards against misuse before the feature can be launched in India.

articles.headline: Auto wholesales maintain positive momentum in June dispatches
articles.summary: Domestic automobile wholesale volumes grew across passenger and two-wheeler segments in June. Maruti Suzuki reported a 24% increase in domestic sales to 1,47,187 units. Mahindra & Mahindra saw a 28% jump in domestic wholesales. However, Hyundai India recorded a 10% decline. The growth is attributed to improved chip supplies and sustained demand for utility vehicles.

articles.headline: Manufacturing PMI slips to 54.2 in June as orders weaken
articles.summary: The HSBC India Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) moderated to 54.2 in June from 57.5 in May. While still indicating expansion, the figure marks the slowest growth in output and new orders since the start of the fiscal year. Job creation also grew at its weakest pace in three months, though input price inflation softened as supply disruptions receded.

articles.headline: JPC to take up Bill seeking to sack jailed PM and CMs
articles.summary: The Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) will meet in July to finalize the 130th Constitution Amendment Bill, 2025. The Bill seeks to bar a Prime Minister, Union Minister, or Chief Minister from continuing in office if they are under arrest or judicial detention for over 30 days in connection with a serious criminal offense. It aims to address governance issues in Delhi.

articles.headline: Jana SFB promoters seek to monetise shares to repay debenture holders
articles.summary: Jana Small Finance Bank (JSFB) promoters, Jana Holdings Ltd and Jana Capital Ltd, plan to sell their stake to repay non-convertible debenture holders. The Bengaluru-based promoters have restructured their debt, extending maturity to June 2026. The bank is currently the fourth largest SFB in India and saw its net profit rise 35% in FY26 to ₹501 crore.

articles.headline: IndiGo introduces entry-level economy fare for ‘lite’ travellers
articles.summary: IndiGo has launched a new 'IndiGo Lite' fare for passengers traveling only with cabin baggage. This lower-priced ticket category allows the airline to unbundle services, where check-in baggage and meals are charged separately. The fare will be available for domestic and select international bookings from July 1 for travel starting July 15.

articles.headline: Super El Nino threat unseen since 1950 is shaping up
articles.summary: Meteorologists warn that a potential 'Super El Nino' is developing in the Pacific, which could trigger worldwide weather extremes. Global sea surface temperatures have reached record highs, with June 2026 seeing the highest temperatures since 1900. This phenomenon threatens to disrupt monsoon patterns and intensify heatwaves and floods globally through the end of the year.

articles.headline: NCDEX reports daily trade of ₹20 crore in RAINMUMBAI derivatives
articles.summary: The National Commodity and Derivatives Exchange (NCDEX) is seeing daily trade of ₹20 crore for its 'RAINMUMBAI' weather derivative. The contract, which tracks rainfall in Mumbai, is being used by insurance firms, construction companies, and retail traders to hedge against weather-related risks. Trade volumes are expected to rise as the monsoon intensifies.

articles.headline: Ola Electric sales double year-on-year in first quarter of FY27
articles.summary: Ola Electric reported sales of 43,719 electric two-wheelers in Q1 FY27, more than doubling the 22,252 units sold in Q1 FY26. The company maintained a 41% market share in the segment. Management attributed the growth to increased consumer awareness, network expansion, and a direct-to-consumer distribution model that reaches over 97% of India's pin codes.

articles.headline: Retail electric car registrations jump 89 per cent in April-June
articles.summary: Retail registrations for electric passenger vehicles surged to 82,737 units in the first quarter of FY27, up from 43,710 units in the same period last year. Tata Motors led the market with a 39% share, followed by MG Motor and Mahindra. The growth indicates that electric mobility is moving from a niche segment into a broader, mainstream consumer phase in India.

articles.headline: AP CM lays foundation for Hero MotoCorp global parts centre in Tirupati
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu laid the foundation stone for Hero MotoCorp’s Global Parts Centre (GPC) in Madanapalle, Tirupati. The project involves an investment of ₹1,500 crore and is expected to create 4,000 direct and indirect jobs. The GPC will have an automated storage system and is designed to handle an annual capacity of 1.2-1.5 million units.

articles.headline: Japan’s $3 billion grant powers India’s Green Hydrogen Mission
articles.summary: Japan has decided to support a green ammonia joint venture in Odisha with a $3 billion grant under its 'Contract for Difference' (CfD) programme. The project, led by Acme Group and IHI Corporation, aims to produce 2.28 lakh tonnes of green ammonia annually for the Japanese power sector. This marks a major commercial endorsement of India’s Green Hydrogen Mission.

articles.headline: Adani Energy Solutions plans ₹10,000 crore QIP for expansion
articles.summary: Adani Energy Solutions has approved a plan to raise up to ₹10,000 crore from institutional investors through a Qualified Institutional Placement (QIP). The funds will be used to strengthen the company’s balance sheet and support its expansion in the power transmission and smart metering sectors. The raising will be done in one or more tranches.

articles.headline: MEIL forays into IT with $50 million joint venture with Analogy
articles.summary: Megha Engineering and Infrastructures Ltd (MEIL) has formed a 50:50 joint venture with US-based Analogy to provide IT and engineering solutions for urban infrastructure. The JV, with an initial revenue target of $55,000 crore, will focus on 'robo-drones' and intelligent security systems to address urban challenges like traffic and security.

articles.headline: Semiconductor push should be followed through with ISM 2.0
articles.summary: The Centre's decision to approve ₹1.25 lakh crore for India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) 2.0 highlights the strategic priority of domestic chip manufacturing. While current investments exceed ₹1.6 lakh crore, experts argue for continued support for local fabless start-ups, advanced lithography access, and R&D to ensure long-term global competitiveness in the silicon supply chain.

articles.headline: FIFA World Cup controversies fade as tournament enters business end
articles.summary: The FIFA World Cup in Miami has seen significant upsets, with nine of the ten participating African teams exiting early. Despite pre-tournament controversies regarding hosting and visas, global attention has shifted to on-field performances. Heavyweights like Germany and the Netherlands have also exited, while Spain and Japan show strong momentum heading into the knock-out rounds.

articles.headline: Roadmap for electric trucks: Charging infra and cheap power are critical
articles.summary: For India to decarbonize its freight sector, which consumes 40% of road transport energy, a roadmap for electric trucks is essential. Experts propose the 'Power Sector Model' involving standardized charging systems, interoperable protocols, and access to clean, low-cost electricity. Initial focus should be on closed-loop operations with high utilization for economic viability.

articles.headline: Calcutta Stock Exchange revival seeks to serve MSMEs and green bonds
articles.summary: A proposed revival strategy for the Calcutta Stock Exchange (CSE) involves positioning it as a specialized hub for MSME listings, carbon credits, and green bonds. By focusing on niche market segments rather than competing directly with NSE or BSE, the CSE aims to provide regional liquidity and serve as a gateway for BIMSTEC economies and maritime economy funds.

articles.headline: India-Japan ties mature into a Special Strategic and Global Partnership
articles.summary: Economic cooperation between India and Japan has reached $53 billion in Official Development Assistance (ODA). New areas of collaboration include semiconductors, AI, and green energy. To further strengthen ties, both nations are looking to renegotiate the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) and explore joint ventures in critical minerals and digital infrastructure.

articles.headline: Modi-Takaichi talks to focus on economy, energy, and supply chains
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi held bilateral talks in New Delhi focused on economic security, emerging technologies, and AI. The leaders discussed enhancing cooperation in energy security and strengthening global supply chains to reduce vulnerabilities. A bilateral framework to promote biogas-powered vehicles is also expected to be launched.

articles.headline: Centre clears ₹14,115 crore highway projects for Delhi and UP
articles.summary: The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs approved two major National Highway projects worth ₹14,115 crore. These include a six-lane tunnel connecting Vasant Kunj to the Dwarka Expressway and a four-lane greenfield highway between Kanpur and Kabrai. The projects aim to improve connectivity between Delhi, Haryana, and Uttar Pradesh, reducing travel time significantly.

articles.headline: AI-enabled cyber threats key risk for financial institutions says RBI
articles.summary: The RBI’s Financial Stability Report warns that AI has increased the sophistication and scale of cyberattacks on financial institutions. NBFCs and banks are vulnerable to AI-generated phishing, automated fraud, and supply chain risks. The report calls for enhanced investment in cybersecurity frameworks and suggests that operational dependence on third-party AI tools is a growing concern.

articles.headline: FPI holdings in Indian equities fall to two-decade low
articles.summary: Foreign Portfolio Investor (FPI) holdings in Indian stocks have dropped to their lowest level in 20 years, now accounting for 16% of the market compared to 20% for domestic institutional investors. The decline is attributed to high valuations and concerns over supply chain disruptions. However, domestic equity markets remain resilient due to strong retail and institutional inflows.

articles.headline: PE-VC investments fall 3% to $6.4 billion in June quarter
articles.summary: Private Equity and Venture Capital (PE-VC) investments in India totaled $6.4 billion in Q2 2026, a 3% decline compared to the previous year. Data center and IT infrastructure sectors attracted the most capital, accounting for $2.7 billion across 149 deals. While large deal counts slowed, experts suggest that new investment opportunities in BFSI and healthcare remain attractive.

articles.headline: KPIT Technologies dives 17% as revenue warning triggers retail sell-off
articles.summary: Shares of KPIT Technologies crashed 17% after the company projected a 1% decline in Q1 FY27 revenue. The warning, attributed to spending cuts by European car makers, led to a loss of ₹4-year market value gains. Nearly 6 lakh retail investors were affected. Analysts have downgraded the stock, citing near-term headwinds in the automotive software sector.

articles.headline: SEBI bars 222 entities and imposes ₹47.7 crore fine in pump-and-dump
articles.summary: SEBI has barred 222 entities from the securities market for seven years for their involvement in a coordinated pump-and-dump scheme. The entities used Telegram and SMS to spread misleading messages and inflate stock prices of five companies before selling at a profit. The regulator has imposed a total penalty of ₹47.7 crore and ordered the disgorgement of unlawful gains.

articles.headline: Quick commerce reshaping strategies and premiumization of FMCG companies
articles.summary: FMCG companies like Britannia, HUL, and Tata Consumer are reshaping their portfolios as quick commerce accounts for up to 25% of their e-commerce sales. This shift is driving demand for premium products and smaller pack sizes. Industry experts note that quick commerce is no longer just for convenience but is becoming a primary channel for monthly grocery replenishment.

articles.headline: Weak monsoon may intensify water stress and threaten kharif sowing
articles.summary: Deficient monsoon rains in June have led to a 13.7% rainfall deficit, threatening kharif crop sowing and reservoirs levels. Areas in Central and North-West India face potential water stress if rainfall does not pick up by mid-July. This could impact agricultural yields and food prices, though the IMD expects a recovery as low-pressure systems develop in the Bay of Bengal.

articles.headline: US limits on Mythos AI access keep foreign firms in limbo
articles.summary: Continued US export controls on Anthropic's 'Mythos' AI model have left international companies unable to access the latest artificial intelligence tools. While global users can access the 'Fable 5' model, the more powerful Mythos remains restricted to a select group of US firms. This has raised concerns about a digital divide and the competitive disadvantage for non-US tech companies.

articles.headline: FSSAI sends notices to energy drink brands over misleading claims
articles.summary: The Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) has issued notices to energy drink manufacturers for making misleading health and therapeutic claims. Brands like Adrenaline Rush and Drink-Gold Boost are being investigated for labeling products with claims like 'vitalises body and mind' without scientific evidence. FSSAI has intensified enforcement against such misbranding.

articles.headline: Lenovo rolls out LOQ game monitors and Tab Plus Gen 2
articles.summary: Lenovo has expanded its gaming and entertainment portfolio in India with the launch of the LOQ monitor series and Tab Plus Gen 2. The flagship monitor features a 27-inch QHD display with a 180 Hz refresh rate. The new tablet is equipped with an eight-speaker system tuned by JBL and Dolby Atmos. Prices for the LOQ monitors start at ₹9,799.

articles.headline: Aarogya Setu 2.0 uses Google Gemma 4 to digitise medical records
articles.summary: The National Health Authority has integrated Google’s Gemma 4 AI model into Aarogya Setu 2.0. The upgrade allows the app to process unstructured health documents, such as lab reports and prescriptions, and convert them into a standard digital format. This is part of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission to help users organize and share their medical history securely.

articles.headline: India's GST Revenue Hits ₹1.95 Lakh Crore in June
articles.summary: India's GST collections rose 13.9% year-on-year to ₹1.95 lakh crore in June 2026, driven by robust domestic activity. Despite global roadblocks and a three-month low in manufacturing activity, UPI transactions grew by 23%, and power consumption rose by 11.6%. The central bank has cut its FY27 growth forecast to 6.6% due to high energy costs and global disruptions.

articles.headline: Udaan Parent Faces Bankruptcy Proceedings Over $170 Million Default
articles.summary: Global creditors have initiated insolvency proceedings in Singapore against Trustroot Internet Pvt Ltd, the parent of B2B e-commerce firm Udaan. The move follows a default on $170 million in convertible notes due June 30. The company, which has seen its valuation drop by 70%, has incurred cumulative losses of approximately ₹13,000 crore since its launch.

articles.headline: Adani and IHC to Invest $11.5 Billion in Aluminium Smelter
articles.summary: Adani Group and Abu Dhabi’s IHC plan to jointly invest $11.5 billion to build an aluminium smelter in Odisha. The plant will have an annual capacity of over 2 million tonnes. This marks one of the largest foreign investments in India’s metals and minerals space, reflecting Adani’s strategy to secure commodity supplies for its expanding infrastructure empire.

articles.headline: SP Group to Issue ₹25,500 Crore Bonds Against Tata Stake
articles.summary: Shapoorji Pallonji Group plans to raise ₹25,500 crore through a bond issue next week, using its 18.37% stake in Tata Sons as collateral. The funds will be used primarily to refinance existing debt. The move follows a recent RBI clarification that effectively bars major NBFCs, including Tata Sons, from remaining privately held if they reach certain asset thresholds.

articles.headline: Government to Propose 30-Day Detention Bill for PM and CMs
articles.summary: The Centre plans to introduce the Constitution (130th Amendment) Bill, 2025, in the upcoming monsoon session of Parliament. The bill seeks to allow the detention of the Prime Minister, Chief Ministers, and other ministers for up to 30 consecutive days without trial in cases involving offenses punishable by five years or more in prison.

articles.headline: Hero MotoCorp to Invest ₹3,200 Crore in Manufacturing Expansion
articles.summary: Hero MotoCorp is scaling up investments to ₹3,200 crore over the next 3-5 years to expand production capacity in Andhra Pradesh and global centers. Executive Chairman Pawan Munjal cited reviving demand for motorcycles and scooters. The company is also developing an electric motorcycle to be showcased at the EICMA show in Milan.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu Government Challenges High Court Ban on Cow Slaughter
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu government has moved the Supreme Court against a Madras High Court order that banned cow slaughter during Bakrid. The state argues that the ban contradicts statutory schemes and that existing laws already ensure animals are not subjected to unnecessary pain. The High Court had previously directed the state to ensure no cow or calf is slaughtered on the holiday.

articles.headline: Tangkhul Naga Houses Torched by Militants Near Manipur Border
articles.summary: Militants suspected to have crossed from Myanmar torched at least 12 houses belonging to the Tangkhul Naga community in Manipur's Kamjong district. Security forces are reviewing the situation as the area remains volatile. Kuki groups have condemned the arson, claiming it was carried out by outfits operating from across the border.

articles.headline: Gujarat High Court Rules Marriages Invalid Without Vedic Rituals
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court has ruled that Hindu marriages performed without the 'Saptapadi' (seven steps) and other traditional rites are 'null and void ab initio.' The court set aside a family court ruling, stating that a marriage certificate alone does not validate a marriage if the mandatory religious rituals were not performed under the Hindu Marriage Act.

articles.headline: Demographic Panel to Study Migration Impact in Border States
articles.summary: A high-level committee on demographic changes, chaired by Justice Prakash Prabhakar, will begin visiting states and Union Territories. The panel aims to study demographic shifts caused by illegal migration and other factors. Home Minister Amit Shah met with the panel to discuss gathering data on migration patterns to assist in policy formulation.

articles.headline: India-Japan Summit Focuses on AI, Chips, and Renewable Energy
articles.summary: The 16th India-Japan Annual Summit will focus on deepening collaboration in artificial intelligence, semiconductors, and clean energy. Japan remains India's fifth-largest investor, with cumulative investments reaching $46.17 billion. Key projects discussed include the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail and a $27 billion bilateral trade target for 2025-26.

articles.headline: Karnataka Government to Audit Free Power Scheme Beneficiaries
articles.summary: The Karnataka government is seeking caste and income details to audit the Gruha Jyoti free power scheme. The move aims to limit subsidy benefits to bona fide residents and manage the ₹34,000 crore annual cost of the program. The opposition BJP has criticized the move, calling it a plot to reduce the number of beneficiaries.

articles.headline: ITC Enters Cola Market with Sugar-Free Coconut Variant
articles.summary: ITC has launched B Natural Select, a carbonated beverage made with tender coconut water, marking its entry into the cola segment. The product is positioned as a healthier alternative to traditional colas. ITC plans to expand its beverage portfolio with more functional variants to compete with global giants Coca-Cola and PepsiCo.

articles.headline: Consumer Firms to Create 5,000 Jobs Amid Rural Growth
articles.summary: Leading FMCG and consumer durable firms, including McDonald’s, L’Oreal, and Haier, are set to create 5,000 new jobs in India over the next two years. The expansion is driven by a resilient domestic market and growing demand in rural areas. Companies are also setting up global capability centers to support international operations.

articles.headline: Electric Vehicle Sales More Than Double in June
articles.summary: Indian electric car and two-wheeler sales more than doubled in June 2026, reaching 30,454 units. The growth is attributed to rising fuel prices, new model launches, and improving charging infrastructure. Two-wheeler registrations rose 62% to 170,426 units for the quarter, with TVS Motor and Bajaj Auto maintaining strong market positions.

articles.headline: New WhatsApp Rules Require Verified Usernames for Security
articles.summary: The government has issued a notice requiring WhatsApp users to set up unique, verified usernames. The move aims to prevent impersonation and cybercrime by allowing users to hide their mobile numbers. Meta has until the end of the week to share details on how it will integrate the feature with existing trust environments like UPI.

articles.headline: Regulatory Concerns Loom Over Zepto’s Dual-Entity IPO Structure
articles.summary: Zepto’s hybrid business structure is drawing regulatory scrutiny ahead of its planned $1 billion IPO. The company operates a marketplace and a separate wholesale unit to comply with FDI rules. Experts suggest this could lead to closer monitoring by the CCI and SEBI regarding intra-group transactions and consumer data privacy.

articles.headline: Anthropic Launches ‘Claude Science’ Platform for Global R&D
articles.summary: AI startup Anthropic has released Claude Science, a specialized AI workbench designed for scientific research. The platform helps researchers analyze large datasets, automate complex workflows, and generate insights in plain language. The tool aims to simplify the research process for scientists across various disciplines.

articles.headline: IT Sector Slump Hits Shares of KPIT, Tata Elxsi
articles.summary: Indian IT stocks fell sharply in mid-2026 as fears of AI-led disruptions and slowing global demand took a toll. Shares of KPIT Technologies plunged 17% after a profit warning, while Tata Elxsi and L&T Technology Services also saw significant declines. Analysts suggest that AI is beginning to offset productivity gains in traditional IT services.

articles.headline: Indian Rupee Falls Past 95 Per Dollar on Fed Hawkishness
articles.summary: The Indian rupee slipped past the 95-per-dollar mark for the first time, driven by high dollar demand and expectations of a hawkish US Federal Reserve policy. Despite RBI interventions, the currency ended at 95.24. Experts anticipate the rupee could fluctuate between 94.7 and 95.5 in the coming days as inflation remains above central bank targets.

articles.headline: Together AI Raises $800 Million in Aramco-Led Funding
articles.summary: Together AI, an infrastructure platform for artificial intelligence, has raised $800 million in a funding round led by Aramco Ventures. The investment values the firm at $8.3 billion. The company plans to use the capital to expand its cloud-based AI services and develop new technologies for private-sector customers globally.

articles.headline: US Approves Export of Anthropic AI Models to Allies
articles.summary: The US Commerce Department has lifted export controls on two advanced AI models from Anthropic, Fable and Mythos, for export to allied nations. The decision followed a review of the models' safety and potential security risks. Anthropic will collaborate with the US government to establish safety standards and prevent misuse of the technology.

articles.headline: Trump Earned $1.4 Billion from Crypto Ventures in 2025
articles.summary: Financial disclosures reveal that former US President Donald Trump earned over $1.4 billion from cryptocurrency ventures in 2025. The income includes sales of digital tokens and management fees from crypto-related projects. Trump's involvement in the industry has grown since his presidency, with his family members also taking on leadership roles in the sector.

articles.headline: Google to Pay $1.5 Billion in Swedish Antitrust Case
articles.summary: A Swedish court has ordered Google to pay $1.5 billion in damages to PriceRunner for antitrust violations. The court found that Google manipulated search results to favor its own comparison-shopping service. The fine is one of the largest ever imposed on a tech company in Europe for competitive malpractices.

articles.headline: June GST mop-up rises 14% on higher import tax
articles.summary: India's Gross Goods and Services Tax (GST) collections grew 14% year-on-year to ₹1.95 lakh crore in June 2026. This growth was driven by a 34.6% surge in GST from imports and a 6.5% rise in domestic transactions. Total collections for the first quarter of FY27 reached ₹6.32 lakh crore, representing an 8.4% growth over the previous year.

articles.headline: Centre notifies minimum wage of ₹300 per day under VB-G Ram G
articles.summary: The Centre has notified new wage rates under the Viksit Bharat - Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) (VB-G Ram G) scheme. The national average wage has been hiked to ₹327.4 per day, with the interim base rate set at ₹300. Some states like Andhra Pradesh and Himachal Pradesh saw increases of up to 25%. The move aims to strengthen rural livelihoods and strengthen the mission.

articles.headline: Rupee closes at three-week low of 95.24 per dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee fell 58 paise to close at 95.24 against the US dollar on Wednesday, its biggest single-day decline in over three weeks. The drop was triggered by aggressive dollar buying from importers and a lack of intervention from the central bank. The rupee was the worst-performing Asian currency of the day as the dollar index strengthened.

articles.headline: Centre slashes LPG prices and reduces commercial gas and ATF rates
articles.summary: The government reduced the price of 19-kg commercial LPG cylinders by ₹183.50 and 14.2-kg domestic cylinders by ₹13. This marks the second price cut in a month, attributed to easing global energy prices. Aviation Turbine Fuel (ATF) prices were also cut by ₹5 to ₹110 per litre in Delhi, providing relief to the airline industry.
